items.title: 'We need all our numbers': Liberal rules out SA upper house presidency bid

children.content: Labor's path to getting legislation through the upper house may get slightly more difficult after Liberal MLC Dennis Hood — who was strongly linked to the upper house presidency role — said he would remain on the floor of parliament.

gallery.altText: An MP stands in a corridor of parliament.

thumbnail.altText: An MP stands in a corridor of parliament.

items.title: Thousands travel to outback Agfair despite fuel costs

children.content: Fuel costs have not stopped thousands of people from descending upon Broken Hill for the biannual Agfair.

image.altText: A young woman smiling at the camera holding a sheep close to her body

items.title: The alternative classroom disengaged students are choosing to be in

children.content: Of the students who have attended the Alternative Suspension program, 67 per cent have received no further suspensions.

image.altText: Art work showing a girl student in a principle's office with her head downcast and scribbles above her head

items.title: Crows claim epic after two goals inside final minute

children.content: Adelaide claims a remarkable one-point victory over Port Adelaide after Hayden Young fires the Dockers to victory over the Bulldogs.

gallery.altText: A group of AFL players celebrate a victory in front of crowd

thumbnail.altText: A group of AFL players celebrate a victory in front of crowd

items.title: Polar blast and snow to follow record May heat over south-east Australia

children.content: Just weeks from winter, and south-east Australia is still enjoying summertime warmth.

image.altText: saftey beach in victoria

items.title: Police confirm identity of remains found in SA bushland

children.content: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

image.altText: Two women and a man stand near a fence with a bouquet of flowers attached to it, with trees behind

items.title: 'Here we go again': Pirie locals concerned over uncertainty of smelter funding

children.content: A $135 million dollar assistance package for Nyrstar smelters in Port Pirie and Hobart ran out on Friday, with negotiations between the company and the South Australian, Tasmanian and federal governments continuing on a new deal.

articles.title: Smelter jobs at stake as government assistance runs dry

children.content: South Australia's premier says there is a "national imperative" to keep smelters running, as a $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar operations at Port Pirie and Hobart expires.

image.altText: An aerial view of the Nyrstar smelter in Port Pirie

gallery.altText: The sign for Ellen Street in the foreground with a large smoke stack from the lead smelter in the background

thumbnail.altText: The sign for Ellen Street in the foreground with a large smoke stack from the lead smelter in the background

items.title: Gas vs beer: How Pocock caused the government a tax headache

children.content: David Pocock was struggling to find cut-through on the issue of gas taxes, but then a question about the beer excise sparked a social media war of words.

articles.title: Albanese says US 'playing a different role' in world

children.content: Anthony Albanese says the United States is "playing a different role" in the world under President Donald Trump, forcing Australia and allies to adjust how they respond to unexpected conflicts.

image.altText: The prime minister makes a point with his hands extended speaking into an ABC microphone in a radio studio.

articles.title: Tie property tax changes to income tax cuts, productivity chief says

children.content: Productivity Commission chair Danielle Wood says there is a "case" for property tax changes, but for it to be serious reform, the economic benefit must flow to workers.

image.altText: A sold sticker on a real estate listing board.

gallery.altText: Senator David Pocock sits at a table with a neutral expression on his face.

items.title: SA government needs certainty on LIV Golf by October, premier says

children.content: The SA Premier says the government is "monitoring" the situation regarding LIV Golf closely, and would need a guarantee of its future in Adelaide by October.

image.altText: Three men smiling and walking, two of them in sportswear and one in a business suit

items.title: From near-misses and cancer to Olympic success, Glaetzer bows out

children.content: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

image.altText: Matt Glaetzer smiles and clenches his fist

items.title: The algal bloom may be 'gone', but many are still grappling with the fall-out

children.content: While testing has shown a dramatic improvement in South Australia's algal bloom situation, industry and experts fear it won't be a quick recovery.

gallery.altText: A mural of a squid painted on the wall of a tackle shop.

thumbnail.altText: A mural of a squid painted on the wall of a tackle shop.

items.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan

children.content: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

image.altText: Close up of cracks in dried lakebed

items.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says

children.content: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue suit and tie is sitting and looking ahead

items.title: Why this cattle farmer is flooding his property with salt water

children.content: More than half a century ago, lush wetlands along the Queensland coastline were cut off from the ocean as farmers built physical barriers to stop salt water flowing into their land. Now, conservation groups and traditional owners are restoring tidal flows and bringing life back to the land.

image.altText: A man stands with a large branch over his shoulders, looking into the distance.

items.title: US to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany amid Trump spat with Merz

children.content: The United States is withdrawing 5,000 troops from NATO ally Germany amid a growing a rift between the nations' leaders over the Iran war.

image.altText: A man with dark suit, white shirt and red tie, smiles. He's standing to the left of the picture against a dark black background.

items.title: Iran's nuclear deal was fragile and it didn't survive Trump

children.content: A secret 2013 meeting inside New York’s Waldorf Astoria helped produce a landmark deal to curb Iran’s nuclear program.
After Donald Trump tore it up, escalation replaced diplomacy — leaving the world once again wondering whether any deal with Iran can truly last.

items.title: Fiji considers state of emergency after suspected drug kingpin murder in custody

children.content: The suspected murder of a Fijian underworld figure held in military custody has sparked a new chapter in the island nation's wrestle with drug-related crimes, with the police and military combining forces for a highly visible "crackdown".

image.altText: Men in military uniforms search a man who is leaning on a car.

items.title: Sabotage and violence alleged on major Brisbane rail project

children.content: Queensland's inquiry into the CFMEU has revealed Cross River Rail was plagued by years of interference. Here are the key takeaways from this week's hearings.

image.altText: Red and black flags with the acronym CMFEU flying in the wind.

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

children.content: The by-election has already had a high turnout of early voters, with the Victorian Electoral Commission saying about half the electorate had already voted by Friday night.

items.title: 1976 in music: The big albums turning 50 this year

children.content: The music industry is always in a state of flux, but what was happening in 1976 was akin to the dinosaurs looking up and seeing a meteor on the way.

image.altText: AC/DC pose for a band photo in 1976.

items.title: This pumpkin soup is comfort food with a twist that takes it to the next level

children.content: Pumpkin soup is one of a few meals with just one main ingredient. This delicious version spices things up with coconut cream and an easy curry-leaf oil.

image.altText: A bowl of blended orange soup. On top are two leaves, dots of oil, a dollop of coconut cream and some black pepper.

items.title: The four women who left 'Australia Street' to try and leave Syria

children.content: Four Australian women, their children and grandchildren are stuck in limbo in Syria after leaving a refugee camp for family members of suspected Islamic State fighters. This is what we know about them.

gallery.altText: A group of women wearing hijabs and face masks, accompanied by a group of children.

thumbnail.altText: A group of women wearing hijabs and face masks, accompanied by a group of children.

items.title: Shift in the Middle East has implications for countries like Australia

children.content: Donald Trump's earlier signalling that he will neither walk away from the war declaring a victory or escalate it, but will try to starve the Iranians out, suggests there is no clear sign of how this war could end.

image.altText: An older man from the UAE in a white robe and head covering looks slightly concerned. The background is blue.

items.title: Iran's race: Break the US blockade or risk its economic lifeline

children.content: Analysis of how much oil Iran is producing, shipping and having to store shows pressure is mounting on Tehran, but while experts agree the US blockade is effective, there are questions about whether it can really force Iran back to the negotiating table.

image.altText: Ships and boats in the Strait of Hormuz, with the sun setting behind hills in the back.

items.title: How young actor's big break ended in court battle with Hollywood star

items.title: Cosmetics will soon cost more. Here's why

children.content: Australians spend about $450 on average on beauty products per year. That could soon rise as petroleum prices increase.

items.title: Man shot at and venues set on fire in latest Melbourne attacks

children.content: A man has been shot at during an aggravated home invasion, and a pub and restaurant were set on fire overnight in Melbourne.

items.title: Man airlifted with life-threatening injuries after caravan park brawl

children.content: A man has been airlifted to hospital with life-threatening injuries after being stabbed during a wild brawl at a caravan park in a small Victorian town.

items.title: 'Butcher of Bosnia' near death, say lawyers asking for early release

children.content: In a submission to a UN court, Ratko Mladić's lawyers say he has suffered a serious medical incident and should be released from The Hague on humanitarian grounds.
